rows.title: JWT tokens signed with HS256 but app uses TLS termination upstream - insufficient key material exposed at edge
rows.description: acme-edge-gateway continues to accept HS256-signed JWTs sourced from the legacy PHP monolith. Upstream TLS termination means the HMAC secret traverses the load balancer in plaintext once per request, and the secret rotation cadence (90d) is not enforced by the gateway policy. RFC 7518 deprecated HS256 for new deployments in favour of RS256/EdDSA.
rows.recommendation: Migrate to EdDSA signing with a per-tenant keypair, rotate to a 32-byte secret >=, and disable the HS256 verifier path once the JWT issuer can hand out only asymmetric tokens. Track with the existing PCI-DSS scope (ADR-005 / ADR-014).

rows.title: EU customer PII inadvertently replicated to us-west-2 via analytics pipeline
rows.description: The acme-recommendations ETL copies row-level events (with email + order history) into a Hive table located in us-west-2 for A/B-test analysis. The region router (ADR-016) only gates the *application* tier - the analytics layer bypassed the region tag and we have ~18,000 EU customers with replicated US-side records. GDPR Article 44 restricts this transfer.
rows.recommendation: Re-tag the Hive external table as eu-west-1, re-route the Spark ETL through the regional Kafka cluster, and add a CI check that any new BigQuery/Hive table carries a region=eu or region=us column with NOT NULL. Open a DPIA with the DPO (per ADR-016).

rows.title: Outdated lodash <4.17.21 transitive dep in acme-catalog exposes prototype pollution
rows.description: acme-catalog (ADR-007, Go service) carries an indirect lodash 4.17.19 dependency via its Node-based admin dashboard subpath. CVE-2019-10744 (prototype pollution) and CVE-2020-8203 (zip slip) are exploitable from unauthenticated JSON merges in the admin SPA.
rows.recommendation: Pin lodash >=4.17.21 in the admin dashboard package.json, enable npm audit --production in the build pipeline, and add the dep vulns to the SecurityPostureCard top_affected_services until the migration to Lit components (Q3 2026) is complete.

rows.title: Envoy mTLS issuer CN pinned to legacy internal CA - new cluster CA rejected
rows.description: Envoy/Istio (ADR-002) was upgraded in May 2026; the new control plane CA's CN does not match the legacy internal CA pinned in 8 of 12 services' envoy.yaml. Result: 4 services silently fall back to plaintext mTLS for east-west traffic. Risk of credential theft if an attacker lands a pod in the same namespace.
rows.recommendation: Update envoy.yaml trust_bundle_volumes across all 12 services, run istioctl experimental precheck, and add a daily grafana alert on outbound plaintext mTLS connections. Roll out alongside the blue/green strategy (ADR-015).

rows.title: Checkout session tokens lack audience binding - token replay across subdomains possible
rows.description: acme-checkout (ADR-004) issues session JWTs without an aud claim, so the same token is accepted by admin.acme.com if a user accidentally pastes it there. Combined with the existing CSRF token (same-site=Lax), an attacker who steals a token via XSS on the marketing site can replay it against /admin/api/* until the 4h TTL expires.
rows.recommendation: Add aud=checkout.acme.com in the JWT issuer, reject tokens without an audience claim, and ship a one-shot revocation list keyed by token id (jti). The 3DS confirmation SSE channel (ADR-004) must also verify audience.

rows.title: Audit log redaction whitelist missing approval.reason field - PII leaked to log aggregators
rows.description: Architecture approval events (ADR-018, F-305) include the free-text reason field (up to 2000 chars) verbatim. The PII redaction pipeline accepts a config-driven field whitelist; the approver.reason field is NOT in the whitelist, so emails, ticket numbers, and sometimes raw card dispute IDs from the customer support team end up in the OLTP audit sink.
rows.recommendation: Add approver.reason to the redaction whitelist with regex=email|cc_last4|ticket_id; the structured reason is still queryable via the JSII SDK API but masked in OLAP exports. Update docs at /docs/security/audit-redaction.md.

rows.title: Next.js Image Optimization bypass allows SSRF via crafted src url
rows.description: acme-storefront (ADR-001) runs Next.js 15 with /_next/image enabled. Crafted src urls pointing at internal IPs (e.g. http://10.0.0.5:8080/admin) bypass the allowList because the regex matches 'acme-cdn.com' but not the resolved IP after a 302 from the CDN. Internal admin endpoints have been probed but no successful exfil yet observed.
rows.recommendation: Disable /_next/image and proxy through CloudFront Functions for image transforms, OR add a domains list that includes only the canonical CNAMEs (no IP literals). Validate against SSRF-2024-Q3 advisory and verify in the SecurityPostureCard weekly trend.

rows.title: OpenTelemetry collector exports bearer token in plaintext to vendor sidecar
rows.description: OTel collector (ADR-013) exports to Datadog via OTLP/HTTP with an Authorization: Bearer header. The bearer token is stored in a Kubernetes Secret but mounted as a plaintext env var into the sidecar, where it shows up in /proc/<pid>/environ for any process that opens the namespace.
rows.recommendation: Mount the token as a projected volume consumed by the collector's TLS config block (token is read once at startup) and remove the env var. Document the token rotation runbook alongside the SecurityPostureCard dependency vulns widget.
